text: The relay was found to be out of tolerance during the functional test.
The calibration certificate had not been issued at the time of the test.
A corrective action must be implemented before the retest.
We need to have the report resubmitted by Friday.
Thank you for joining, Camille. Let me state the finding first. During the functional test, the safety relay was found to be out of tolerance, eleven milliseconds against a specified five to seven.
Yes, we saw that. But honestly, João, it is a minor deviation. The unit works. I would not hold the shipment for this.

A deviation is approved before the work. This was found after it, on a safety relay, and the calibration certificate had not been issued at the time of the test. I am afraid that is a non-conformance.
The certificate was issued the following Monday. And the calibration itself was performed by our subcontractor, not by us.
To some extent I understand your position. However, our contract is with you, so the root cause has to be identified on your side, and a certificate issued after the test does not cover the test.
So what exactly do you need from us before you release the unit?

Three things by Friday. The root cause, a corrective action plan, and the relay retested with a valid certificate. We need to have the report resubmitted through the approval workflow, and until the hold point is closed, sign-off cannot be granted.
Understood. I will have the unit retested on Thursday and I will send you the certificate myself. You are not going to move on this, are you?
It would appear that the deviation was caused by the calibration drift.
Sign-off cannot be granted while the hold point remains open.

João, it is Camille. I said I would call you before Friday, and I would rather you heard this from me than read it in a report. We have the root cause. A reference instrument in our calibration laboratory had drifted, and it had not been recalibrated on schedule. The drift was identified on Tuesday, and every unit tested against that instrument since April is now being reviewed. Yours was not the only one. The corrective action plan will be sent to you tomorrow, the relay will be retested on Thursday, and the certificate will be issued before the retest, not after it. Now the part I have to ask, and you are free to say no. If the retest passes, I would like the inspection release on Friday and not the following week, because the shipment is booked and the rework has already cost us three days. And one more thing. On Monday I called it a minor deviation. I should not have. If our positions had been reversed, I would not have signed it off either, and I would have held my ground exactly the way you did.

Before you raise that non-conformance report, three things, because engineers get these wrong more often than anybody else. First. The passive is not cold English and it is not weak English. It is the only register that survives a disagreement. If you write, your technician calibrated the relay badly, you have written an accusation, and an accusation can be denied, defended and argued about for three weeks. If you write, the relay was found to be out of tolerance during the functional test, you have written a finding. The person is out of the sentence and the fact is in it, and a finding does not get argued with. It gets closed. Every standard on your shelf is written this way, and there is a reason. Second. Do not agree a corrective action before the root cause has been identified. The relay reading eleven milliseconds is a symptom. The reason it read eleven is the root cause, and until somebody has found it, whatever you agree on will fail a second time, at your cost and on your schedule. Ask for the root cause first. Always. Third, and this is the one that costs money. Never let the supplier choose the word. A deviation is approved before the work is done. Anything found after the work is a non-conformance, and it has to be corrected or formally waived. The moment you accept the word deviation for something that was found, not agreed, you have accepted the rework, and you will pay for it. State the finding, ask for the cause, name the date, and let them tell you how they will fix it. That is the whole meeting.
The relay was found to be out of tolerance during the functional test.
The calibration certificate had not been issued at the time of the test.
A corrective action must be implemented before the retest.
We need to have the report resubmitted by Friday.
Sign-off cannot be granted while the hold point remains open.
The root cause has not been identified yet, so the corrective action is premature.
It would appear that the deviation was caused by the calibration drift.
